Category_Description: Everyday dry pantry essentials including rice, lentils, spices, flour, cooking oils, nuts, sugar, and salt. Core staples for South Asian, African, and Caribbean cooking.
Ready-to-eat snack foods and sweet treats including crisps, namkeen, biscuits, cookies, chocolates, candies, popcorn, and traditional Indian sweets and mithai.
All drinkable products including teas, coffees, soft drinks, fruit juices, energy drinks, malt drinks, syrups, cordials, and water. Covers both hot and cold drinks.
Fresh, unprocessed fruits and vegetables including root vegetables, leafy greens, gourds, pods, exotic and specialty produce. Products sold fresh without packaging or preservation.
Frozen meat, poultry, fish and seafood, as well as chilled and ambient ready-to-cook and marinated products. Includes convenience meal kits and semi-prepared foods.
Flavour-enhancing accompaniments including pickles, chutneys, cooking sauces, ketchup, pastes, purees, jams, honey, spreads, and vinegar. Used to season, marinate, or accompany meals.
Refrigerated dairy products including milk powder, condensed milk, cheese, paneer, butter, yoghurt, and eggs. Core protein and dairy ingredients for cooking and consumption.

Non-food household and personal use products including toiletries, hair care, skin care, cosmetics, household cleaning supplies, laundry products, disposables, packaging, and over-the-counter medicines.
Baked goods and grain-based meal staples including bread, bakery items, noodles, pasta, breakfast cereals, oats, and canned and tinned food products.
